jokes.text: You spent close to $20,000 on a couch you never owned.
jokes.setup: Characters casually mention renting a couch for $25/week for 15 years
jokes.punchline: Dennis does the math and reveals the absurd total they paid
jokes.explanation: Great setup/payoff structure with Dennis being the voice of reason while others remain oblivious. The absurd math reveals character stupidity perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You spent close to $20,000 on a couch you never owned.

jokes.text: That seems high.
jokes.setup: Dennis calculates they spent $19,500 on a rental couch
jokes.punchline: Mac's completely inadequate response to the shocking revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement comedy. Mac's casual dismissal of losing thousands shows his complete disconnect from reality.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That seems high.

jokes.text: I have glued my hand to the door, so they can't physically remove me.
jokes.setup: Dee announces she's being evicted
jokes.punchline: Her completely ineffective protest method
jokes.explanation: Absurd logic presented matter-of-factly. Dee's misguided activism is perfectly in character and visually ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I have glued my hand to the door, so they can't physically remove me.

jokes.text: Charlie, we don't want to hear it.
jokes.setup: Charlie tries to pitch his investment idea
jokes.punchline: Immediate dismissal before he can even start
jokes.explanation: Sets up Charlie's running frustration. Simple character beat showing the gang's dismissive attitude.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Charlie, we don't want to hear it.

jokes.text: So maybe what we would need to do is lower our wages. I get it.
jokes.setup: Frank explains inflation economics
jokes.punchline: Mac eagerly agrees to lower his own wages
jokes.explanation: Mac's enthusiasm for self-destructive economic policy shows his complete lack of understanding while thinking he's being smart.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: So maybe what we would need to do is lower our wages. I get it.

jokes.text: That's money talk.
jokes.setup: Characters discuss incomprehensible economics
jokes.punchline: They treat gibberish as sophisticated business talk
jokes.explanation: Establishes a running gag. Characters' fake sophistication about business they don't understand at all.

dialogue.line: That's money talk.

jokes.text: But I'll give you 50 cents for a buck.
jokes.setup: Discussion of dollar devaluation
jokes.punchline: Dennis offers a deal that makes no mathematical sense
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis thinks he's making a smart deal while actually offering to lose money. Good character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: But I'll give you 50 cents for a buck.

jokes.text: An inflatable couch?
jokes.setup: Mac reveals his furniture solution
jokes.punchline: The absurdly cheap/inappropriate furniture choice
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy of grown adults using pool furniture as living room furniture. Sets up the episode's central prop.

dialogue.line: An inflatable couch?

jokes.text: These are very fancy. Are they? Three dollars?
jokes.setup: Mac proudly presents 'Fancy Nuts'
jokes.punchline: Dennis's deadpan observation of their obvious cheapness
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deflation of Mac's pride in his terrible purchase. Dennis as voice of reason.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: These are very fancy.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Are they? Three dollars?

jokes.text: Oh, well, you can't inflate this any further 'cause it'll pop.
jokes.setup: Dennis uses 'inflate' metaphorically
jokes.punchline: Mac takes it completely literally about the furniture
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Mac's literal thinking. The wordplay setup works because it's so in character.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh, well, you can't inflate this any further 'cause it'll pop.

jokes.text: I just kept blowing and blowing and blowing, and just... boom.
jokes.setup: Mac explains he already popped two couches
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic description of destructive over-inflation
jokes.explanation: Sets up Mac's inflation obsession. Physical comedy of him destroying furniture through enthusiasm.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I just kept blowing and blowing and blowing, and just... boom.

jokes.text: You mean to tell me we've been living here all this time, and you never told me we had a bathroom?
jokes.setup: Frank discovers a toilet in their shared space
jokes.punchline: The revelation that they've been living in squalor unnecessarily
jokes.explanation: Great escalation revealing Charlie's bizarre living situation logic. Frank's outrage is justified and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: You mean to tell me we've been living here all this time, and you never told me we had a bathroom?

jokes.text: We piss in cans, Charlie.
jokes.setup: Frank's shock about the hidden bathroom
jokes.punchline: The horrifying revelation of their actual bathroom habits
jokes.explanation: Perfectly grotesque and economical. Reveals the depth of their squalor in four words. Peak Sunny grossness.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: We piss in cans, Charlie.

jokes.text: I wasn't trying to be crypto about it, it's just, like, Frank, I just never liked sleeping in a room with an empty room behind me.
jokes.setup: Frank demands to know why Charlie hid the rooms
jokes.punchline: Charlie's completely irrational spatial anxiety explanation
jokes.explanation: Pure Charlie logic - completely nonsensical but delivered with total conviction. His specific phobias are perfect character work.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I wasn't trying to be crypto about it, it's just, like, Frank, I just never liked sleeping in a room with an empty room behind me.

jokes.text: I don't want to live in a maze, you know?
jokes.setup: Charlie explains why he doesn't use the extra room
jokes.punchline: His characterization of a two-bedroom apartment as a 'maze'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Charlie logic. Two rooms = maze shows his complete inability to handle basic living situations.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I don't want to live in a maze, you know?

jokes.text: I'm gonna have to get a butler.
jokes.setup: Frank complains about walking to answer the door
jokes.punchline: His immediate leap to hiring household staff for a tiny apartment
jokes.explanation: Frank's wealthy background showing through - his solution to minor inconvenience is immediate luxury staffing.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I'm gonna have to get a butler.

jokes.text: Wait, so you truly have no reason to be sleeping with Frank.
jokes.setup: Everyone discovers Charlie has been hiding usable space
jokes.punchline: Dennis pointing out the unnecessary intimate living arrangement
jokes.explanation: Dennis as voice of reason pointing out the obvious weirdness everyone else accepts. Good cringe comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Wait, so you truly have no reason to be sleeping with Frank.

jokes.text: Charlie, you mean we've been living in a two-bedroom with a bathroom, and you never said anything about it?
jokes.setup: Frank already discovered a hidden bathroom
jokes.punchline: Now he discovers Charlie's been hiding an entire bedroom too
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity - Charlie hasn't just hidden amenities but entire living space

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Charlie, you mean we've been living in a two-bedroom with a bathroom, and you never said anything about it?

jokes.text: That's a lot of blowing.
jokes.setup: Mac finishes inflating furniture
jokes.punchline: Dennis's dry observation about the amount of inflation required
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor. Sets up the running gag about Mac's inflation activities.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: That's a lot of blowing.

jokes.text: What happened to your lips?
jokes.setup: Mac has been inflating furniture
jokes.punchline: Visual reveal of Mac's swollen/damaged lips from blowing
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup. Mac's dedication to the task has visible consequences. Sets up his worsening condition.

dialogue.line: What happened to your lips?

jokes.text: That is rancid. Did you get a bad one? Well, they're all bad.
jokes.setup: Frank tries the 'Fancy Nuts'
jokes.punchline: Revelation that all the cheap nuts are inedible
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from one bad nut to all nuts being terrible. Mac's penny-wise, pound-foolish shopping exposed.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: That is rancid.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Did you get a bad one?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Well, they're all bad.

jokes.text: Kim Kardashian's backside, the early years.
jokes.setup: Dennis presents a business pitch with a 'lookbook'
jokes.punchline: It's just photos tracking Kim Kardashian's body changes
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Dennis business logic. Treating celebrity body changes as market research is brilliantly ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Kim Kardashian's backside, the early years.

jokes.text: The Kardashians' net worth has inflated in direct proportion to their booties.
jokes.setup: Dennis explains his inflation theory
jokes.punchline: Connecting economic inflation to body part inflation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay connecting the episode's economic theme to celebrity culture. Dennis's pseudo-intellectual analysis is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: The Kardashians' net worth has inflated in direct proportion to their booties.

jokes.text: I'm in. Yeah. You're in? You don't need to hear the whole pitch?
jokes.setup: Dennis prepares elaborate business pitch
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately agrees without hearing any details
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - builds up Dennis's elaborate pitch only to have Frank skip it entirely. Shows Frank's real motivations.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'm in.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You're in? You don't need to hear the whole pitch?

jokes.text: People are doing it to call attention to injustices or some shit. I don't know. Either way, I'm locked in.
jokes.setup: Dee explains why she glued herself to the door
jokes.punchline: Her complete lack of conviction about the cause she's protesting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee - adopting activism without understanding or caring about the cause. Her casual 'some shit' undermines any nobility.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: People are doing it to call attention to injustices or some shit. I don't know. Either way, I'm locked in.

jokes.text: He's jacking off into a magazine.
jokes.setup: Dennis explains why Frank doesn't want to be bothered
jokes.punchline: Blunt description of Frank's private activities
jokes.explanation: Classic Sunny crudeness delivered matter-of-factly. Dennis's casual mention of inappropriate behavior is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: He's jacking off into a magazine.

jokes.text: I'm not investing in them because I believe in them. I'm investing in them because I don't believe in them.
jokes.setup: Charlie asks why Frank invested in Mac and Dennis
jokes.punchline: Frank reveals his predatory lending strategy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal of expectation. Frank's logic is perfectly cynical and reveals his true predatory nature. Great structure and character work.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I'm not investing in them because I believe in them. I'm investing in them because I don't believe in them.

jokes.text: It's your goddamn wheezing. Am I wheezing? Are you wheezing? You sound insane.
jokes.setup: Dennis can't sleep
jokes.punchline: Mac's increasingly labored breathing from nut allergies
jokes.explanation: Continues Mac's deteriorating health subplot. Good escalation of his physical comedy suffering.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It's your goddamn wheezing.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Am I wheezing?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Are you wheezing? You sound insane.

jokes.text: You ever have a pine nut? Which one is... A fancy tree?
jokes.setup: Charlie describes foraging nuts from trees
jokes.punchline: Mac's continued focus on 'fanciness' even for foraged nuts
jokes.explanation: Mac's inability to drop the 'fancy' fixation even when discussing wild forage shows his materialistic mindset.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You ever have a pine nut? Which one is... A fancy tree?

jokes.text: Frank's not the only one who can be a predatory lender. I say we pivot.
jokes.setup: Dennis learns about Frank's predatory lending
jokes.punchline: His immediate decision to become equally predatory
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis - instead of being outraged by predatory behavior, he wants to copy it. Shows his complete amorality.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Frank's not the only one who can be a predatory lender. I say we pivot.

jokes.text: Go for it, bitch! We got bulletproof vests on! Oh, no, I don't. I don't have a bulletproof vest.
jokes.setup: Frank boldly challenges an armed tenant
jokes.punchline: Dee's panicked realization she's unprotected
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection and escalation. Frank's false bravado immediately undermined by Dee's vulnerability. Good tension and release.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Go for it, bitch! We got bulletproof vests on!

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Oh, no, I don't. I don't have a bulletproof vest.

jokes.text: What? You're my landlord? Yeah, I got tired of paying your rent all those years, so I bought the building.
jokes.setup: Dee discovers Frank is her landlord
jokes.punchline: Frank's casual admission of his scheming
jokes.explanation: Great reveal showing Frank's long-term manipulation. His matter-of-fact delivery of terrible behavior is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: What? You're my landlord?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Yeah, I got tired of paying your rent all those years, so I bought the building.

jokes.text: I don't have a pool, so... Oh, no, no, no, I see, I see the confusion. No, we're talking about for inside.
jokes.setup: Customer hears 'inflatable couch'
jokes.punchline: Natural assumption it's pool furniture vs. their indoor furniture plan
jokes.explanation: Good reality check moment. Normal person's confusion highlights how absurd their business plan is.

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: I don't have a pool, so...

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh, no, no, no, I see, I see the confusion. No, we're talking about for inside.

jokes.text: You pay full price for your couch? That's outrageous.
jokes.setup: Customer doesn't rent his couch
jokes.punchline: Dennis treating normal behavior as outrageous
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis - his warped worldview makes normal furniture purchasing seem crazy. Good role reversal.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You pay full price for your couch? That's outrageous.

jokes.text: I'm making a bet that the SEC's not gonna regulate. Of course, regulation's gonna come in at some point, it always does, but right now it's completely unregulated.
jokes.setup: Charlie finally gets to pitch his business idea
jokes.punchline: He delivers surprisingly sophisticated financial jargon
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character reversal. Charlie, who's been dismissed all episode, suddenly demonstrates real financial knowledge. Great payoff.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I'm making a bet that the SEC's not gonna regulate. Of course, regulation's gonna come in at some point, it always does, but right now it's completely unregulated.

jokes.text: Charlie, did you take all of my money and invest it in a box full of Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtle Pies?
jokes.setup: Charlie's confident business talk
jokes.punchline: Reveal that his 'investment' is nostalgic snack cakes
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to opening scene. Charlie's sophisticated pitch collapses into childhood nostalgia. Brilliant structure and misdirection.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Charlie, did you take all of my money and invest it in a box full of Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtle Pies?

jokes.text: Charlie, did you take all of my money and invest it in a box full of Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtle Pies?
jokes.setup: Charlie promised a lucrative investment opportunity
jokes.punchline: His 'investment' is 30-year-old children's snack cakes
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that transforms opening throwaway line into major plot point with Charlie's insane logic

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Charlie, did you take all of my money and invest it in a box full of Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtle Pies?

jokes.text: Are they made from real turtle? Probably.
jokes.setup: Frank asks about turtle pie ingredients
jokes.punchline: Charlie's casual acceptance of probably eating turtle
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan delivery. Charlie's casual 'probably' to eating turtle shows his complete disconnection from food safety.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Are they made from real turtle?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Probably.

jokes.text: 'Cause I bought the last remaining ones there are. No... Nice work.
jokes.setup: Frank threatens to buy pies elsewhere
jokes.punchline: Charlie reveals he cornered the entire market
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Charlie actually did create artificial scarcity. Frank's respect shows Charlie finally succeeded at business.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: 'Cause I bought the last remaining ones there are.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: No... Nice work.

jokes.text: That's inflation, Frank.
jokes.setup: Frank objects to Charlie's demands
jokes.punchline: Charlie uses Frank's own economic lesson against him
jokes.explanation: Perfect thematic callback and character moment. Charlie finally understands and weaponizes the economics lesson. Brilliant economy and structure.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: That's inflation, Frank.

jokes.text: Are you still eating the nuts?
jokes.setup: Mac is clearly having severe allergic reactions
jokes.punchline: He's still eating the thing that's killing him
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Mac's priorities being completely wrong. His self-destructive behavior is peak Mac stupidity.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Are you still eating the nuts?

jokes.text: I think we'll take you to the hospital, and they'll have nuts, and you can die there.
jokes.setup: Mac needs medical attention for allergic reaction
jokes.punchline: Dennis's casual suggestion Mac can continue his self-destruction at the hospital
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy at its finest. Dennis's matter-of-fact acceptance of Mac's death wish is perfectly callous and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I think we'll take you to the hospital, and they'll have nuts, and you can die there.
